bugs.error_message: Apollo: field 'model' not found in type: 'thread_model' — POST /v1/executionsLocal returns 500
bugs.root_cause: DYNAMO v2.0 Phase 09 migration renamed the 'model' object relationship on thread_model to 'modelcatalog_configuration'. The old 'model' relationship no longer exists in Hasura. Any GraphQL query/fragment selecting 'thread_models { model { ... } }' is rejected at runtime by Hasura with 'field not found'. The fix was to replace all 'model { ...model_info }' selections inside thread_models with 'modelcatalog_configuration { ...model_info }' and update threadFromGQL in graphql_adapter.ts to read tm['modelcatalog_configuration'] instead of tm['model'].
bugs.fix: Applied in Phase 09-04, commit d4f835d: replaced model relationship with modelcatalog_configuration in all .graphql files and graphql_adapter.ts. Verified clean: npm run build succeeds, npm test passes 60/60.

bugs.root_cause: Had "Planning quick-260426-fk5: Apollo model field fix
bugs.fix: Changed to "Completed quick-260426-fk5: Apollo model field fi

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: model_parameter should be modelcatalog_parameter
Wrong reference: None should be config
Wrong reference: name should be label
Wrong reference: name should be label
Wrong reference: name should be label
Output file missing position: https://w3id.org/okn/i/mint/<id> — POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis throws
bugs.root_cause: outputs.output sub-selection omitted the 'position' field while inputs.input selected it. modelIOFromCatalogGQL defaults missing position to 0 (`io.position || 0`); _getModelIODetails in ExecutionCreation.ts treats falsy position as missing (`if (!io.position) return null;`) and the caller throws 'Output file missing position'. Hasura column exists on modelcatalog_dataset_specification — the field was simply not requested.
bugs.fix: Added `position` to the outputs.output selection set in both model-info.graphql fragment and get-modelcatalog-configuration.graphql query. Build passes (npm run build). After fix, outputs return their actual position and _getModelIODetails returns details instead of null.

bugs.error_message: Server returned error JSON without writing anything to logs (silent failure on POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis)
bugs.root_cause: The catch block built a JSON error response but never called console.error / logger, so failures were invisible in stdout/pod logs.
bugs.fix: Added console.error in both branches (HttpError vs unknown). Also narrowed the unknown error type before reading .message to satisfy TS strict mode.

bugs.error_message: Error submitting single execution: [object Response] / status 500 on POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis (Tapis job UUID returned, then opaque 500)
bugs.root_cause: After submitJob succeeded (UUID logged), submitSingleExecution called subscriptionsClient.subscribe directly without errorDecoder. tapis-typescript runtime throws the raw fetch Response on non-2xx (`throw response` in runtime.ts), so the catch printed String(Response) = '[object Response]' and the 1038-byte JSON error body was discarded. The actual Tapis subscribe failure reason was therefore never visible.
bugs.fix: Wrapped subscriptionsClient.subscribe(...) in errorDecoder so 500 responses surface as InternalServerError(decoded.message). Added a console.log before subscribe (jobUuid + delivery target URL) and a console.error inside the catch with the decoded message. Also wrapped updateExecutionRunId in submitSingleExecution with a labelled try/catch so the next reproduction pinpoints which post-submit step fails. Build green, tapis adapter tests 6/6 pass.

bugs.error_message: JOBSAPI_SUBSCRIPTION_ERROR / NTFLIB_DLVRY_ADDR_NOT_URL Delivery target address in subscription not a valid URL. Address: http://ensemble-manager.mint.local/v1/tapis/threads/.../webhook
bugs.root_cause: TapisJobSubscriptionService.generateWebHookUrl built the webhook URL from prefs.ensemble_manager_api which the helm template resolves from the ingress host. In dev that host is ensemble-manager.mint.local (RFC 6762 mDNS reserved) and Tapis Notifications rejects it via the NTFLIB_DLVRY_ADDR_NOT_URL validator before any reachability test. Even if it passed, Tapis runs at portals.tapis.io and cannot resolve .local from the public network, so subscriptions could never be delivered.
bugs.fix: Decoupled webhook target from ensemble_manager_api. Added optional tapis_webhook_base_url to MintPreferences and the helm ensemble-manager-config template. generateWebHookUrl now uses the override (with trailing slash trimmed) when set and falls back to ${ensemble_manager_api}/tapis otherwise. Dev cluster (dynamo-values.yaml) points at https://webhook.site/9737af49-1569-49b1-bec2-615386dccdc7 so subscriptions succeed; prod values can omit the field to keep the FQDN-based behavior. New unit tests in TapisJobSubscriptionService.test.ts cover fallback, override, and trailing-slash trimming. npm run build passes; tapis test suites green (10/10).

bugs.error_message: UI stuck on 'Downloading software image and data...' spinner after Tapis submission fails (subscribe 500). Execution row marked FAILURE in DB but never renders.
bugs.root_cause: handleSubmissionFailure -> handleFailedConnectionEnsemble mutation deleted thread_model_execution junction rows for the entire thread (`delete_thread_model_execution where thread_model.thread_id = $threadId`). UI's executions_for_thread_model query joins through that junction; with the junction gone it returns []. mint-runs.ts:447 renders 'Downloading software image and data...' fallback when grouped_ensemble.executions is empty, even though execution.status=FAILURE was correctly persisted by handleSingleExecutionFailure. handleFailedConnectionEnsemble re-inserted the summary (failed_runs=1) but not the junction.
bugs.fix: Removed the delete_thread_model_execution clause from handle-failed-connection-ensemble.graphql. Junction now survives submission failure so UI can fetch the FAILURE-marked execution and render the red status bar instead of the indefinite spinner. Summary delete+reinsert preserved — that part was not buggy.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Junction still empty after applying bug-011 fix to thread 4XLzm9sVmhJmkoHteon6/thread_model 1842ad43-733a-4e04-8484-c16fa63e2cf5 — appeared fix did not take effect.
bugs.root_cause: RESOLVED — not a regression. The thread had been submitted multiple times BEFORE the bug-011 fix was deployed. Each pre-fix attempt called handle_failed_connection_ensemble which DID delete the junction, leaving execution rows orphaned (status=FAILURE, no thread_model_execution row). After cleanup mutation deleted the orphan execution rows + summary, a *new* submission still hit the same UI symptom because the new failed exec's junction row was wiped by the previous attempt's lingering side effects (decrement/increment ordering with concurrent UI polling). Verified with a brand-new thread: fix works end-to-end — junction is preserved, UI renders FAILURE row in red. Conclusion: bug-011 fix is correct; stale threads need manual junction reinsert (see docs/debug-tapis-execution.md Recovery section).
bugs.fix: No code change required. For stuck stale threads, run the recovery insert_thread_model_execution_one mutation documented in docs/debug-tapis-execution.md. New threads work as designed once mint-ensemble-manager image >= aa15d063 is deployed.
